source_file: Santander AM -Market Outlook 2026 Climbing towards new highs.pdf
global_growth_2026_why: AI and supportive policy keep the global cycle extending
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: World 3.3%
us_gdp_2026_why: U.S. growth slows but remains supported by healthy balance sheets
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: We estimate GDP growth of 2% in 2025 and around 1.6% in 2026.
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: German stimulus offsets tariff drag and lifts eurozone growth
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: leading to a GDP growth that we estimate of around 1% in 2026.
china_gdp_2026_why: China keeps a gradual slowdown under fiscal support
china_gdp_2026_raw_wording: We forecast GDP growth of 4% in 2026 compared to 4.9% in 2025.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: Fed makes one more cut toward a neutral stance
fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: we anticipate that the Fed will end 2025 with interest rates at 3.5%/3.75%, and that in 2026 there will be another 25 bps cut, bringing rates down to 3.25%/3.5%.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_why: Santander still sees one final ECB cut in early 2026
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_raw_wordi: we consider it likely that there will be a further rate cut to 1.75% in the first quarter of 2026 if business confidence does not recover somewhat from its current low level.
eur_usd_2026_why: Santander expects EUR/USD to stay range-bound in 2026
eur_usd_2026_raw_wording: We expect the EUR/USD exchange rate to remain within a 1.12-1.20 range in 2026
equities_overall_why: Equities are explicitly the core of the strategy.
equities_overall_raw_wording: Equities remain at the core of our strategy for 2026. The global backdrop supports this constructive view, consolidating equities as the main growth engine in diversified portfolios.
equities_geography_europe_why: Eurozone equities are supported by strong earnings expectations.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: From near double-digit earnings growth estimates in the Eurozone to supportive domestic conditions, equities combine global macro tailwinds and local support.
equities_geography_japan_why: Santander sees Japanese equities supported by nominal growth, resilient earnings, and better corporate governance.
equities_geography_japan_raw_wording: Japan approaches 2026 with a combination of sustainable inflation, resilient corporate earnings, and structural reforms that underpin a constructive view on risk assets.
equities_geography_em_why: EM and LatAm are explicit parts of the positive equity mix.
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: The momentum of Latin American markets and attractive dividend yields in Emerging Asia support the constructive equity view. China may also offer tactical opportunities.
equities_geography_china_why: Santander views China as a tactical opportunity supported by policy easing and targeted fiscal support.
equities_geography_china_raw_wording: China may offer tactical opportunities supported by policy shifts.
fixed_income_overall_why: Santander sees carry and normalization as fixed-income opportunities.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: The path toward neutral policy rates progresses at different speeds across geographies. In the Eurozone, strong corporate fundamentals and historically attractive carry make credit a stable source of return for 2026.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
Eurozone IG is explicitly preferred.
We maintain a positive view on Eurozone investment-grade credit for 2026. Yields remain attractive and offer an additional premium over sovereign debt.
fixed_income_duration_why: Santander favors an income-oriented bond stance with moderate rather than aggressive duration exposure.
fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: investors should favor an income-oriented strategy built on coupon accumulation, moderate duration, and limited credit exposure
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: Selective sovereign opportunities are part of the bond allocation.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: The divergence in policy opens opportunities in UK and Brazilian sovereign bonds, where carry combines with our expectation of rate cuts.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_why: Corporate credit is a stable return source in the Eurozone.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_raw: In the Eurozone, strong corporate fundamentals and historically attractive carry make credit a stable source of return for 2026.
fixed_income_segment_inflation_linked_wh: Santander explicitly includes inflation-linked sovereign exposure in the opportunity set.
fixed_income_segment_inflation_linked_ra: Nominal and inflation-linked UK government bonds still offer the highest yields among G7 economies.
currencies_usd_why: Santander expects the dollar to stay broadly range-bound in 2026 rather than trend sharply in either direction.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: The USD is expected to remain broadly stable in 2026.
commodities_gold_why: Gold is the preferred hedge in the commodity book.
commodities_gold_raw_wording: We remain positive on gold. Gold remains on an upward trend and continues to be the preferred hedge against geopolitical and inflation risks.
commodities_oil_why: Oil is capped by oversupply in the base case.
commodities_oil_raw_wording: In oil, we anticipate an oversupplied market in 2026, with Brent prices in the 55–60 USD per barrel range.
commodities_industrial_metals_why: Supportive demand exists, but the upside is capped by supply and China.
commodities_industrial_metals_raw_wordin: Demand linked to the energy transition and infrastructure investment should remain supportive, but rising supply and slower growth in China will likely cap the recovery potential of industrial metals.
themes_ai_why: AI capex is central to the house thematic framework.
themes_ai_raw_wording: Corporate investment in AI infrastructure, semiconductors, and the cloud is surging to record levels and is now large enough to contribute nearly half of U.S. real GDP growth.
themes_energy_transition_why: Energy transition and infrastructure remain supportive thematic drivers.
themes_energy_transition_raw_wording: Demand linked to the energy transition and infrastructure investment should remain supportive for industrial metals.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitics is framed primarily as a live downside risk that can reprice regions and asset classes.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: Trade, technology, and capital are being redirected by industrial policy, tariffs, and sanctions, fragmenting global supply chains.

global_growth_2026_why: World growth stays resilient into 2026
us_gdp_2026_why: U.S. growth slows but stays near trend
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: Eurozone growth remains modest in 2026
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Eurozone 1.4 1.1 1.4 1.0
china_gdp_2026_why: China keeps growth near its target range
em_growth_2026_why: Emerging markets retain a clear growth premium
us_inflation_2026_why: U.S. inflation stays above target in 2026
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: Eurozone inflation undershoots the ECB target
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: Fed cuts toward neutral and then pauses
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_why: ECB stays on hold through year-end 2026
eur_usd_2026_why: UBS sees the euro holding near 1.20
equities_overall_why: Explicit call to add to equities.
equities_overall_raw_wording: We expect global equities to rise by around 15% by the end of 2026, and we think investors underallocated to stocks should add to equities.
equities_geography_us_why: US remains the core equity engine.
equities_geography_us_raw_wording: Our positive views on US tech and US equities are core drivers, and in the US we favor technology, utilities, and health care.
equities_geography_europe_why: Europe is explicitly upgraded to Attractive.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: We upgrade European equities to Attractive, with a particular focus on the Eurozone.
equities_geography_japan_why: UBS stays constructive on Japan thanks to supportive policy, earnings recovery, better returns on equity, and lower valuations.
equities_geography_japan_raw_wording: Japan. While political uncertainty may contribute to volatility in the near term, we expect more accommodative policies under the new government to support the market over the medium term.
equities_geography_em_why: Emerging market equities are part of the preferred opportunity set.
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: Beyond the US, we also see opportunities in Europe, Japan, and China. Strong liquidity, earnings, and retail flows should sustain momentum for Chinese, Asian, and emerging market equities.
equities_geography_china_why: China is singled out within Asia as a preferred equity market.
equities_geography_china_raw_wording: In Asia, we favor China, and China’s tech sector stands out as a top global opportunity.
fixed_income_overall_why: Rates coming down shift the focus back to portfolio income.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: A robust fixed income allocation provides stability and income while managing risk.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
Quality / higher-grade bonds are treated as core portfolio ballast.
We recommend diversifying across developed market government bonds, corporate and emerging market bonds, with quality bonds offering attractive yields.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: UBS is cautious on high yield because spreads are tight, but still sees a role for select high yield inside diversified fixed income strategies.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: We are more cautious on riskier parts of fixed income like high yield given very tight spreads. Nonetheless, we see merit in diversified fixed income strategies.
fixed_income_duration_why: Prefers intermediate duration rather than an extreme duration stance.
fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: We believe an average duration of five to seven years balances risk and return for long-term investors, especially when rates are expected to change gradually.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: Government bonds are held for stability.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: We recommend diversifying across developed market government bonds for stability.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_why: Corporate bonds are part of the income allocation.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_raw: We recommend diversifying across corporate bonds for higher income.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_why: EM bonds are part of the diversified income mix.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_raw_wording: We recommend diversifying across emerging market bonds for higher income.
currencies_overall_why: UBS frames FX as an active source of return and prefers long positions in the euro and other higher-yielding currencies against the dollar.
currencies_overall_raw_wording: We favor long positions in the euro, Australian dollar, and Norwegian krone versus the USD. Our preference is for high-yielding currencies.
currencies_usd_why: US dollar is the funding currency they prefer against.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: Currency strategy matters. We favor the euro and Australian dollar over the US dollar, as US rate cuts may weigh on the greenback.
currencies_eur_why: Euro is a preferred currency versus the dollar.
currencies_eur_raw_wording: Currency strategy matters. We favor the euro and Australian dollar over the US dollar.
commodities_overall_why: UBS says commodities are attractive for 2026 as supply constraints and rising demand support the complex.
commodities_overall_raw_wording: Commodities look attractive for 2026. Supply constraints and rising demand support energy, metals, and agriculture; precious metals remain effective diversifiers.
commodities_gold_why: Gold is explicitly recommended as a hedge allocation.
commodities_gold_raw_wording: Gold has historically been an effective portfolio hedge during market stress, inflation fears, or geopolitical uncertainty. We believe a modest allocation can enhance diversification and buffer against systemic risks.
commodities_industrial_metals_why: Industrial metals, especially copper and aluminum, should benefit from tight supply and AI and energy-transition demand.
commodities_industrial_metals_raw_wordin: Looking ahead to 2026, both copper and aluminum should continue to benefit from improving fundamentals.
themes_ai_why: AI remains a core growth engine.
themes_ai_raw_wording: AI and technology have been key drivers of global equity markets. Strong capex and adoption should fuel further gains in 2026.
themes_healthcare_why: Healthcare is one of the explicitly preferred sectors.
themes_healthcare_raw_wording: In the US, we favor technology, utilities, and health care.
themes_energy_transition_why: Energy transition and power/resource demand are explicit structural drivers.
themes_energy_transition_raw_wording: Commodities are supported by long-term trends like the global energy transition. Within the asset class, we see particular opportunities in copper, aluminum, and agricultural commodities.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitical risk is a central hedge rationale rather than a pure growth theme.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: Gold has historically been an effective portfolio hedge during market stress, inflation fears, or geopolitical uncertainty.
themes_other_why: Longevity is a named structural theme beyond AI.
themes_other_raw_wording: Opportunities span AI’s enabling, intelligence, and application layers, as well as power and resources and longevity.

global_growth_2026_why: AI investment and policy support keep global growth resilient
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: We forecast growth of around 2.7% supported by the ongoing AI-driven investment cycle and proactive policy responses in key regions.
us_gdp_2026_why: AllianzGI expects the U.S. economy to stay resilient but slow to below-potential growth in 2026.
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: The US economy is expected to remain resilient, though growth will slow to around 1.5-2%, slightly below potential.
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: AllianzGI expects a moderate cyclical eurozone recovery supported by incomes, easier policy, and German fiscal spending.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Europe is poised for a moderate cyclical recovery, with GDP growth expected at 1-1.5% in 2026.
us_inflation_2026_why: AllianzGI expects U.S. inflation to remain above target in 2026 because of tariff effects.
us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: US inflation is set to rise above 3%.
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: AllianzGI expects eurozone inflation to stay subdued and below target.
eurozone_inflation_2026_raw_wording: Inflation is projected to remain below 2%.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: AllianzGI expects the Fed to keep cutting in 2026 into the low-to-mid 3% range.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: The US Federal Reserve is expected to continue cutting rates in 2026, bringing the federal funds target range to 3.25-3.50%.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_why: Soft inflation allows one more ECB cut
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_raw_wordi: Inflation is projected to remain below 2% enabling the European Central Bank to cut rates by 25 basis points to 1.75% in the first half of the year.
equities_overall_why: Overall equity positioning favors a diversified ex-US opportunity set.
equities_overall_raw_wording: Europe, China and India offer diversified, attractively priced equity markets, alongside significant policy support. Together, these three regions form a compelling equity allocation framework for navigating the shifting global order.
equities_geography_us_why: US equities are treated cautiously because of valuation and concentration risk.
equities_geography_us_raw_wording: We currently view US equities as richly priced. Combined with near record levels of concentration, a selective investment approach is required.
equities_geography_europe_why: Europe is preferred on valuation and breadth grounds.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: We believe European equities are, across sectors, currently more attractively priced than many US counterparts, giving them appeal in terms of both value and growth.
equities_geography_em_why: Emerging-market equity exposure is supported through India-led diversification.
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: In Asia, India currently stands out as a market with high potential due to multiple tailwinds and a broader, more diversified set of investment opportunities.
equities_geography_china_why: China is framed as a contrarian equity opportunity with policy support.
equities_geography_china_raw_wording: China’s equity market remains deep and attractively priced, as well as under-owned by foreign investors, presenting contrarian opportunities for long-term capital flows.
fixed_income_overall_why: The fixed-income backdrop is described as benign rather than defensive only.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: This growth and inflation combination underpins an overall benign environment for fixed income and creates opportunities for selective duration and credit positioning across regions.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
AllianzGI explicitly favors investment-grade credit and crossover bonds over lower-quality spread product.
We continue to like investment grade credits and crossovers.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: AllianzGI sees high yield as expensive and less compelling than higher-quality credit.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: High yield spreads are expensively priced in our view.
fixed_income_duration_why: AllianzGI sees developed-market duration as a useful source of resilience in a benign fixed-income backdrop.
fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: Developed markets duration offers resilience, while emerging market debt can provide yield enhancement and diversification.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_why: EM debt is explicitly named as a preferred diversifier and yield source.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_raw_wording: Developed markets duration offers resilience, while emerging market debt can provide yield enhancement and diversification.
currencies_usd_why: The dollar is treated as a headwind to concentrated US exposure.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: A potentially weaker dollar may prompt global investors to rethink high exposures to US assets.
currencies_eur_why: AllianzGI expects the euro to strengthen relative to peers as Europe faces less political pressure on monetary policy.
currencies_eur_raw_wording: The ECB and BoE are major central banks not under the yoke of heavy political pressure, strengthening the euro and sterling relative to their peers.
currencies_jpy_why: JPY is framed as a scalable safe-haven diversifier.
currencies_jpy_raw_wording: The Japanese yen could further consolidate its safe-haven status, supported by political stability and the Bank of Japan’s shift in response to higher core inflation.
commodities_gold_why: Gold remains a core hedge even after strong performance.
commodities_gold_raw_wording: Gold remains a key diversification tool for us, though it may have reached near-term peak levels. We continue to hold positions.
themes_ai_why: AI is treated as a broad structural growth theme rather than a narrow US trade.
themes_ai_raw_wording: The US and China continue to lead the AI revolution, with spillovers to other regions. A broadening of tech spend outside the US could sustain growth and usher in a truly global AI revolution.
themes_energy_transition_why: Energy transition is a named multi-asset opportunity set.
themes_energy_transition_raw_wording: Funding the energy transition and digital infrastructure will create opportunities across asset classes, including for investors with the ability to hold long-term and illiquid assets.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitics is presented primarily as an elevated cross-asset risk.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: Geopolitical risks remain elevated, particularly around Russia and East Asia.
themes_defense_sovereignty_why: European strategic autonomy and defense spending are explicit investable themes.
themes_defense_sovereignty_raw_wording: In its quest for increased strategic autonomy, Europe is embracing a more expansive fiscal policy and significant spending initiatives, especially in Germany, on defense and military hardware.
themes_other_why: Digital infrastructure is a separate structural theme beyond the standard buckets.
themes_other_raw_wording: Digital infrastructure. Data centres, fibre networks and 5G connectivity form the backbone of Europe’s digital sovereignty. We continue to see opportunities, both within equity and debt, going into 2026.

us_gdp_2026_why: Domestic demand and investment keep U.S. growth resilient
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Nel 2026, ci aspettiamo che la crescita su base trimestrale si attesti rispettivamente all'1,7% nel primo trimestre, 1,8%, 2,0% e 2,1% nei tre trimestri successivi, con la media annua al 2,0%.
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: German fiscal support gradually lifts eurozone activity
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: coerenti con una stima dell'1,0% per l'intero 2026.
china_gdp_2026_why: China stabilises growth with continued support
china_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Il PIL dovrebbe espandersi a un ritmo del 4,9% nel 2025 e stabilizzarsi al 5% nel 2026.
us_inflation_2026_why: Core disinflation continues through 2026 in the U.S.
us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: Nel 2026, riteniamo che i prezzi al consumo core si attesteranno in media al 2,8% nel primo semestre e 2,4% nel secondo, con la media annua al 2,6%.
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: Euro area disinflation continues on softer wages and energy
eurozone_inflation_2026_raw_wording: 1,8% nel secondo semestre coerenti con una media annua all'1,8%.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: Fed turns more accommodative as disinflation resumes
fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: Ci aspettiamo che il tasso sui Fed funds scenda leggermente al di sotto della fascia di neutralita (2,75-3%) entro la fine del terzo trimestre 2026.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_why: ECB makes one final cut and then pauses
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_raw_wordi: Ci aspettiamo che la BCE effettui un ulteriore taglio a marzo e porti il tasso di deposito a 1,75%.
equities_overall_why: Anima remains constructive on global equities overall.
equities_overall_raw_wording: Le prospettive per i mercati azionari globali restano positive: ci aspettiamo che gli indici principali registrino nuovi massimi, principalmente con il traino della crescita degli utili.
equities_geography_us_why: The US remains the preferred developed-market equity exposure.
equities_geography_us_raw_wording: Privilegiamo gli Stati Uniti rispetto agli altri Paesi sviluppati.
equities_geography_europe_why: Europe is supported by local fiscal stimulus and still expected to perform positively.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: Pensiamo che anche Europa e Giappone registreranno performance positive, con il sostegno dei piani di stimolo fiscale locali.
equities_geography_japan_why: Japan is also expected to benefit from local fiscal support.
equities_geography_japan_raw_wording: Pensiamo che anche Europa e Giappone registreranno performance positive, con il sostegno dei piani di stimolo fiscale locali.
equities_geography_em_why: Anima is explicitly overweight emerging Asia.
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: Manteniamo un sovrappeso sull’Asia emergente, sostenuta dall’esposizione alla tecnologia e alle catene del valore legate all’intelligenza artificiale, nonché dal probabile indebolimento del dollaro.
equities_style_growth_why: Growth remains the preferred style tilt.
equities_style_growth_raw_wording: Privilegiamo i comparti ciclici rispetto ai difensivi, con un bias più costruttivo sui titoli Growth.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
Euro IG remains a core credit exposure even with tighter valuations.
Corporate Euro Investment Grade: restiamo investiti, ma manteniamo un approccio cauto, privilegiando i segmenti di mercato in cui i rischi sono meglio remunerati.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: High yield is tactical and selective rather than a broad overweight.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: Il credito High Yield in euro ha registrato dinamiche peculiari. Gli sviluppi restano selettivamente favorevoli, ma i rischi paiono asimmetrici e non adeguatamente remunerati.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: Anima is moderately constructive on developed-market sovereign bonds because elevated yields should still support returns in 2026.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: Siamo moderatamente costruttivi sui mercati obbligazionari dei Paesi sviluppati nel 2026. Ci aspettiamo che le performance dei titoli governativi siano sostenute dai livelli storicamente elevati dei rendimenti.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_why: Corporate credit remains supported by resilient macro and easing policy.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_raw: Il 2025 è stato un altro anno positivo per il mondo del credito. I trend macro e di politica monetaria sono rimasti favorevoli e permane l’impostazione nella calibrazione dell’esposizione al rischio credito/tasso.
currencies_usd_why: A weaker dollar is part of the EM preference.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: Manteniamo un sovrappeso sull’Asia emergente, sostenuta anche dal probabile indebolimento del dollaro.
commodities_gold_why: Gold remains supported as a macro and geopolitical hedge.
commodities_gold_raw_wording: Il rally dell’oro è stato sostenuto dalle persistenti tensioni geopolitiche, dall’incertezza sul contesto macroeconomico e politico, dalla debolezza del dollaro e dalle attese di allentamento monetario negli Stati Uniti.
themes_ai_why: AI is treated as a core structural growth driver.
themes_ai_raw_wording: Suggeriamo di aumentare l’esposizione ai settori legati alla tecnologia e a quelli più direttamente esposti allo sviluppo dell’intelligenza artificiale, che costituisce una delle principali leve di crescita strutturale per i mercati emergenti.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitical tension remains a live portfolio risk factor.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: Le persistenti tensioni geopolitiche restano uno dei principali fattori a supporto dell’oro e di volatilità sui mercati.

eurozone_gdp_2026_why: BNP expects eurozone growth to regain momentum in 2026, helped by fading uncertainty and German fiscal support.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: The Eurozone economy is likely to accelerate in 2026.
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: BNP expects further eurozone disinflation, with inflation undershooting the official target later in the horizon.
eurozone_inflation_2026_raw_wording: We see scope for further disinflation over the next couple of years.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: BNP expects meaningful Fed easing in 2026, taking U.S. policy rates below 3%.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: For 2026 this suggests substantial reductions in US interest rates to below 3%.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_why: BNP expects the ECB to keep easing through 2026 if inflation undershoots target.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_raw_wordi: We expect inflation to fall below target in 2027, which will prompt the European Central Bank to deliver a couple more interest rate cuts by the end of 2026.
equities_overall_why: BNP's summary view stays constructive on equities, with Europe and selected emerging markets highlighted as the most compelling opportunities.
equities_overall_raw_wording: European equities offer compelling value... In emerging markets, nations with strong technology sectors stand to benefit from lower US yields and a weaker dollar.
equities_geography_europe_why: Europe is explicitly highlighted as the better value market.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: European equities offer compelling value, particularly as the region pursues greater strategic autonomy, potentially offering a broadening set of opportunities for forward-looking investors.
equities_geography_em_why: EM equities benefit from the rates and dollar backdrop.
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: Low US Treasury yields, and a weaker US dollar, are typically positive factors for emerging market equities.
equities_geography_china_why: China is preferred within EM tech because of breadth and relative insulation.
equities_geography_china_raw_wording: Of the three countries with the largest technology sectors — South Korea, Taiwan, and China — the latter offers greater diversity. We believe China’s technology sector has the additional advantage of being somewhat protected from current trade tensions.
fixed_income_overall_why: The overall bond stance is constructive, especially for credit.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: Fixed income markets should benefit from continued central bank easing in 2026. The core scenario is positive for credit markets notwithstanding tight credit spreads and signs of increased leverage.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
BNP is constructive on higher-quality credit thanks to attractive yields and healthy issuer fundamentals.
European investment grade bonds boasting yields of circa 3% to 3.5% ... European issuers' credit quality remains in good health.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: High yield is investable, but only with explicit selectivity.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: We believe opportunities exist across defensive sectors, real estate, and high yield, but a nimble approach is required.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: Government bonds are constrained by fiscal-risk concerns.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: Despite this benign rate outlook, sovereign markets will remain at risk of increased investor concern on the fiscal side.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_why: Corporate credit is preferred for carry and income.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_raw: Prevailing yields in credit markets are attractive and should deliver attractive income-driven total returns.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_why: EM debt remains one of the more attractive total-return bond segments.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_raw_wording: High yield and emerging market bonds continue to be interesting from a total return perspective.
currencies_usd_why: BNP assumes a softer dollar backdrop in its EM allocation logic.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: Low US Treasury yields, and a weaker US dollar, are typically positive factors for emerging market equities.
themes_ai_why: AI remains a structural earnings and innovation theme.
themes_ai_raw_wording: AI’s transformative potential persists, with robust fundamentals supporting valuations and innovation driving new opportunities across sectors.
themes_energy_transition_why: Energy transition is treated as a durable investment theme despite policy noise.
themes_energy_transition_raw_wording: The technological and societal shifts underlying the transition will persist and continue to demand investors’ attention. The Asia-Pacific region is a leader in driving the global energy transition, creating potentially significant investment opportunities.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Trade friction and geopolitical fragmentation remain explicit risks in BNP's 2026 outlook.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: The global economy enters 2026 with surprising resilience, buoyed by a rebound in activity, despite ongoing trade frictions.
themes_defense_sovereignty_why: Defense and strategic autonomy are presented as long-duration investment themes.
themes_defense_sovereignty_raw_wording: Europe now allocates billions annually to defence, industrial resilience, and key technology sectors. We believe this is a structural trend that is here to stay.
themes_other_why: Sustainability and climate solutions are explicit structural themes beyond the core taxonomy.
themes_other_raw_wording: Sustainability remains a central focus, with European and Asian investors leading the charge on green bonds, decarbonisation, and climate solutions.

global_growth_2026_why: World growth stays close to 2025 levels
us_gdp_2026_why: AI investment offsets weaker labour and demand
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: Defence and infrastructure spending support Europe
china_gdp_2026_why: China stays resilient on AI and productivity push
us_inflation_2026_why: Tariffs and migration restrictions keep inflation elevated
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: Cooling labour market prompts limited Fed cuts
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_why: ECB remains on hold as growth improves
eur_usd_2026_why: Slower US momentum and Fed easing weaken the dollar
equities_overall_why: The house view is explicitly overweight equities.
equities_overall_raw_wording: Au tournant de l’année, nous maintenons notre position surpondérée en actions. Sans resserrement monétaire de la Fed ou récession, les investisseurs ne doivent pas sous-pondérer les actions.
equities_geography_europe_why: Europe is supported by German fiscal stimulus and improving momentum.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: Europe remains robust. The eurozone is expected to continue growing steadily, and rising fiscal spending, particularly in Germany, is expected to provide further tailwinds in 2026.
equities_size_small_cap_why: Small caps are named as catch-up beneficiaries of broader market breadth.
equities_size_small_cap_raw_wording: We expect market breadth to pick up again next year. This would give many sectors that have been weak due to high interest rates the potential to catch up, especially small caps.
fixed_income_overall_why: Constructive on credit even while cautious on sovereigns.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: Overall, we are therefore cautiously positive about the corporate bond markets for 2026. Moderate confidence extends beyond government bonds.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
Higher-quality credit is treated as attractive versus sovereign bonds.
Corporate bonds offer interesting returns, and more cautious investors may consider covered bonds with strong credit ratings and a yield advantage over high-quality government bonds.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: High yield is supported, albeit selectively.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: The high-yield segment has seen structural improvements in terms of collateralisation and interest rate sensitivity. Here, we see selective opportunities.
fixed_income_duration_why: The firm is negative on duration in core sovereign markets.
fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: We expect yields on ten-year German government bonds to rise moderately and yields on US Treasuries to rise somewhat more sharply. Neither German nor American government bonds are likely to generate attractive returns.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: Government bonds are explicitly unattractive for euro investors.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: Neither German nor American government bonds are likely to generate attractive returns. For euro investors, safe government bonds remain unattractive across the board.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_why: Corporate credit is the preferred bond area.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_raw: Overall, we are cautiously positive about the corporate bond markets for 2026. In the credit segment, temporary corrections are opportunities to selectively increase risk exposure.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_why: The preferred bond mix includes frontier and EM-style local currency debt.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_raw_wording: In bonds, we prefer covered bonds, high-yield bonds and niche segments such as local currency bonds from frontier markets or catastrophe bonds.
currencies_usd_why: Berenberg expects continued medium-term dollar weakness.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: The greenback will remain under pressure in the medium term. We therefore anticipate that the EUR/USD exchange rate will move toward the 1.20 handle in 2026.
currencies_jpy_why: Berenberg expects the yen to recover moderately as the Bank of Japan continues tightening.
currencies_jpy_raw_wording: This time, however, the JPY is likely to be helped mainly by the BoJ continuing its monetary tightening.
commodities_gold_why: Gold remains a strategic portfolio component.
commodities_gold_raw_wording: The precious metal is likely to remain an important component of portfolios in an environment of ongoing geopolitical uncertainty, increased inflation volatility, fiscal dominance and continued central bank purchases.
commodities_industrial_metals_why: Industrial metals are supported by tightening supply-demand balances.
commodities_industrial_metals_raw_wordin: Incremental purchases from Europe and a recovery in demand from the US are likely to lead to a growing supply deficit for copper in 2026. The same applies to aluminium.
themes_ai_why: AI remains a central structural growth theme.
themes_ai_raw_wording: Artificial intelligence remains a global growth driver thanks to massive capital investments in data centres and energy infrastructure.
themes_energy_transition_why: Data-center and power infrastructure spending is a favored structural theme.
themes_energy_transition_raw_wording: Massive investments in data centres and energy infrastructure will ensure that artificial intelligence remains a key growth driver.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitical risks are explicitly expected to remain elevated and drive volatility spikes in 2026.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: (geo)political risks are likely to remain elevated and volatility spikes are likely to recur.
themes_defense_sovereignty_why: Defense spending is a core European support theme.
themes_defense_sovereignty_raw_wording: Fiscal stimulus will boost the German economy in 2026, with additional government spending particularly in the defence sector.

us_gdp_2026_why: Candriam expects U.S. growth to slow after being supported by AI-linked investment.
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Cette croissance devrait ralentir.
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: Candriam expects a more supportive European policy mix in 2026 thanks to rearmament, German fiscal stimulus, and easier monetary policy.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Le plan ReArm Europe, la relance budgetaire allemande et l'assouplissement monetaire creeront un policy mix encore plus favorable en 2026.
us_inflation_2026_why: Candriam expects U.S. inflation to remain persistent even as the labor market slows.
us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: la Reserve federale se trouve prise en etau entre une inflation persistante et un marche du travail en deceleration.
equities_overall_why: Candriam is constructive but explicitly selective rather than broad-beta bullish.
equities_overall_raw_wording: En 2026, les marchés devront encore arbitrer entre promesses de croissance, fragilités persistantes et nécessité d’une gestion sélective.
equities_geography_europe_why: Europe is supported by fiscal expansion, easier policy, and rearmament.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: Le plan ReArm Europe, la relance budgétaire allemande et l’assouplissement monétaire créeront un policy mix encore plus favorable en 2026.
equities_geography_china_why: China benefits from tech and policy support, but internal fragility remains.
equities_geography_china_raw_wording: Pékin s’est imposée dans la bataille commerciale, mais la Chine peine encore à sortir de la déflation. Ses marchés ont rebondi, tirés par les valeurs technologiques et les soutiens publics.
fixed_income_overall_why: Candriam favors credit opportunity only with clear selectivity and stress-testing.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: La sélectivité demeure essentielle tant sur le marché du crédit public que sur le privé. Le segment du crédit privé pourrait se confronter à ses limites: moindre transparence, liquidité réduite et affaiblissement des protections contractuelles.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: ND
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: Candriam argues government bonds have lost part of their traditional safe-haven role because stock-bond correlations have turned positive.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: Les correlations positives entre actions et obligations reduisent le role traditionnel des emprunts d'Etat comme valeur refuge.
commodities_gold_why: Gold is no longer treated as a clean hedge.
commodities_gold_raw_wording: L’or, longtemps perçu comme une protection, s’apparente désormais davantage à un actif spéculatif.
themes_ai_why: AI is transformative, but Candriam is explicit about valuation sensitivity and bubble risk.
themes_ai_raw_wording: L’ère de l’intelligence artificielle: entre révolution et illusion. Une nouvelle révolution industrielle est en marche, mais les valorisations interrogent et l’euphorie actuelle n’est pas sans rappeler la bulle des années 2000.
themes_healthcare_why: Healthcare is one of Candriam’s clearest preferred themes.
themes_healthcare_raw_wording: Le secteur de la santé sera l’un des grands bénéficiaires de cette transformation. Avec des valorisations raisonnables et un regain d’activité en fusions-acquisitions, la santé pourrait redevenir un pilier de performance à long terme.
themes_energy_transition_why: Energy transition is explicitly linked to enabling AI at scale.
themes_energy_transition_raw_wording: L’essor de l’IA s’accompagne d’un défi énergétique colossal. Répondre à cette demande impose d’accélérer la transition énergétique: sans énergie verte et abondante, pas de révolution durable.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Candriam treats geopolitical tension and fragmentation as a persistent 2026 risk backdrop.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: En 2026, les marches devront encore arbitrer entre promesses d'innovation et tensions geopolitiques.
themes_defense_sovereignty_why: Security, sovereignty, and rearmament are treated as durable structural themes.
themes_defense_sovereignty_raw_wording: Les chaînes de valeur se redessinent au nom de la sécurité et de la souveraineté. Le plan ReArm Europe et la recomposition des alliances créent un nouveau cadre d’investissement.
themes_other_why: Impact and resilience are explicit portfolio-building themes beyond the base grid.
themes_other_raw_wording: L’intégration de tendances structurelles — intelligence artificielle, santé, transition énergétique, investissement à impact — renforce la robustesse des portefeuilles.

global_growth_2026_why: Credit Mutuel expects growth momentum to remain positive in 2026.
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: Growth momentum remains positive.
us_gdp_2026_why: US growth is supported by fiscal stimulus and still-resilient activity.
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Fiscal policies remain highly expansionary ... positive effects in 2026
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: Eurozone growth remains limited but above potential thanks to German investment support.
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Growth will remain limited but above potential growth, thanks in particular to investment plans in Germany.
us_inflation_2026_why: US inflation is expected to stabilize slightly above 3% in 2026.
us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: Inflation should stabilize slightly above 3% in 2026.
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: Eurozone inflation is expected to sit slightly below the 2% target in 2026.
eurozone_inflation_2026_raw_wording: Inflation is expected to be slightly below the 2% target in 2026.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: The Fed is expected to be constrained in future cuts by resilient activity and inflation pressures.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: The Fed will be constrained in future cuts due to activity rebound and inflationary pressures.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_why: The ECB is expected to keep policy unchanged in 2026 unless the macro backdrop deteriorates sharply.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_raw_wordi: The ECB is therefore likely to maintain the status quo in 2026
fixed_income_overall_why: The whole document is constructive on money markets, IG, and HY carry.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: Overall, we remain positive on money market funds
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
IG credit is supported by attractive carry and strong technicals.
High demand in the European IG credit market ... should provide technical support
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: Credit Mutuel expects HY performance to stay supported by carry and low defaults.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: carry is expected to remain the primary driver of High Yield performance in 2026
fixed_income_duration_why: They keep a positive duration stance focused on the belly of the curve.
fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: We maintain a positive duration stance ... favoring the belly of the curve (3-7 years).
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: They are overweight Germany and constructive on Italy and Spain in sovereigns.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: We are overweight Germany, positive on Italy and Spain, and underweight France
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_why: IG corporate spreads are still supported by demand and carry.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_raw: Credit spreads on investment grade bonds continued to hit record lows
themes_ai_why: AI demand supports parts of the macro backdrop, but AI bubble risk is explicitly flagged.
themes_ai_raw_wording: demand driven by the AI theme is strong ... Concerns about the AI bubble

global_growth_2026_why: DWS expects a solid but not classical global upswing in 2026, with growth still sufficient to support capital markets.
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: We do not expect a classical global upswing in 2026 and 2027 but we believe economic growth should provide a sufficiently solid foundation.
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: Europe inflation moves back to target
eurozone_inflation_2026_raw_wording: In Europe, the inflation rate should fall to an annual average of 2% in 2026
eur_usd_2026_why: DWS expects a structurally softer U.S. dollar over time rather than giving a clean point target.
eur_usd_2026_raw_wording: In our view, the U.S. dollar has partially lost its status as a safe haven and is likely to weaken over time.
equities_overall_why: DWS remains constructive on the overall equity backdrop.
equities_overall_raw_wording: We remain optimistic and expect solid returns in the vast majority of asset classes in the coming twelve months. So long as the economic cycle remains intact, the positive momentum in equity markets should, in our view, continue.
equities_geography_us_why: The US is relatively de-emphasized because of concentration and AI-related uncertainty.
equities_geography_us_raw_wording: Because of the uncertainty attached to AI, we are favoring the less technology-heavy European and Japanese equity markets over the U.S. market.
equities_geography_europe_why: Europe is preferred as a less concentrated alternative to the US.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: Because of the uncertainty attached to AI, we are favoring the less technology-heavy European and Japanese equity markets over the U.S. market.
equities_geography_japan_why: Japan is preferred for the same diversification and valuation reasons as Europe.
equities_geography_japan_raw_wording: Because of the uncertainty attached to AI, we are favoring the less technology-heavy European and Japanese equity markets over the U.S. market.
fixed_income_overall_why: The fixed-income preference is constructive through credit rather than sovereign duration.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: Corporate bonds remain attractive in our view, despite very low credit spreads, thanks to the supportive macro environment and high demand.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
DWS is constructive on corporate bonds overall and only asks for selectivity in high yield, implying a stronger preference for higher-quality credit.
Corporate bonds remain attractive in our view, despite very low credit spreads, thanks to the supportive macro environment and high demand.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: High yield is investable, but only selectively.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: Corporate bonds remain attractive in our view, though we favor a selective approach in the high-yield segment.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_why: Corporate bonds are the clearest preferred bond segment in the note.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_raw: Corporate bonds remain attractive in our view, despite very low credit spreads, thanks to the supportive macro environment and high demand.
currencies_usd_why: DWS explicitly expects dollar weakness.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: In our view, the U.S. dollar has partially lost its status as a safe haven and is likely to weaken over time.
commodities_gold_why: Gold is a direct beneficiary of geopolitical hedging demand.
commodities_gold_raw_wording: Geopolitics could weigh on equity markets, but it should boost gold and oil.
commodities_oil_why: Oil has geopolitical upside but a weaker structural supply-demand backdrop.
commodities_oil_raw_wording: Geopolitics could weigh on equity markets, but it should boost gold and oil. For oil, however, we currently continue to anticipate structural oversupply.
themes_ai_why: AI is supported, but DWS explicitly treats it as two-sided.
themes_ai_raw_wording: We also have a constructive view of the AI boom, though AI’s ability to surprise, negatively or positively, was once again demonstrated at the start of the year.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitics is explicitly identified as a major portfolio risk.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: We see three major risks: AI, sovereign debt sustainability and geopolitics.
themes_other_why: Sovereign debt sustainability is a named macro risk theme.
themes_other_raw_wording: We see three major risks: AI, sovereign debt sustainability and geopolitics.

us_gdp_2026_why: EFG still sees the U.S. as the strongest advanced economy
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: We believe that the US is set to be the strongest advanced economy in 2026 and indeed the latest IMF projections also reflect this view.
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: Lower ECB rates and German fiscal support lift Europe
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: The lagged impact of lower European Central Bank rates will provide a tailwind to regional growth in 2026 and it is expected that the German economy will receive a significant boost from fiscal spending.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: EFG doubts the Fed will cut much below 3%
fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: we doubt the Federal Reserve will reduce rates much below 3% given strong real growth and inflation which is still sticky.
equities_geography_us_why: EFG keeps the US as the preferred developed-market equity exposure.
equities_geography_us_raw_wording: Action for investors: within developed markets, remain focused on the US in terms of growth prospects.
equities_geography_europe_why: EFG expects renewed interest in European equities as German stimulus, defense spending, green investment, and lower rates improve the backdrop.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: We believe a renewed excitement for European and Swiss equities is likely.
equities_geography_em_why: EFG remains constructive on selective EM equity exposure.
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: Emerging markets with strong domestic growth prospects should remain attractive for investors.
equities_geography_china_why: China is still treated as the dominant EM growth and technology platform.
equities_geography_china_raw_wording: Despite structural headwinds, China is still set to be the predominant emerging economy. The rapid adoption of new technology, notably in green energy, artificial intelligence and electrification, remains an important strength.
fixed_income_overall_why: The bond stance is constructive through carry rather than deep duration risk.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: There remains value in shorter- to mid-term duration exposure. This environment allows investors to borrow at the short end of the yield curve and invest further out to capture attractive carry opportunities.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: ND
fixed_income_duration_why: EFG prefers shorter-to-intermediate duration rather than long-duration risk.
fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: There remains value in shorter- to mid-term duration exposure.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: Government-bond exposure is constrained by fiscal-sustainability risk.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: There are several markets — notably France and the UK — for which fiscal sustainability will be questioned in 2026. Pledges to bring government finances under control may pacify the sharks for a while but when confidence evaporates bond markets will look vulnerable once again.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_why: Local EM sovereign debt is one of the clearer preferred bond segments.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_raw_wording: Look for opportunities in high-yielding emerging sovereign markets where there is the prospect of capital gains as long-term yields decline.
currencies_usd_why: The base case assumes a softer dollar.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: In a general environment of a stable-to-weakening US dollar, the risk of currency depreciation seems contained for 2026.
themes_ai_why: AI is central, but EFG explicitly frames it with financing and payoff questions.
themes_ai_raw_wording: Technological advancement, most notably artificial intelligence, and the significant investment required to develop the data infrastructure of the future will be central issues. Key questions will include how these investments will be financed and what the long-term benefits will be.
themes_healthcare_why: EFG explicitly favors innovative healthcare exposure, especially smaller biotech-led drug development opportunities.
themes_healthcare_raw_wording: We favour exposure to smaller, innovative companies that are in a position to lead new drug development rather than larger, established healthcare companies.
themes_energy_transition_why: EFG links EM and China strength to green-energy and electrification themes.
themes_energy_transition_raw_wording: The rapid adoption of new technology, notably in green energy, artificial intelligence and electrification, remains an important strength.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitical fragmentation remains a core risk theme.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: Continued rivalry between the US and China, the forging of new alliances and bifurcation are the key geopolitical themes we see for 2026.
themes_defense_sovereignty_why: EFG sees higher European defense spending and strategic restructuring as a durable investment support for 2026.
themes_defense_sovereignty_raw_wording: The EU will take further restructuring and reform steps in 2026 ... pressure from the US to increase defence spending.

global_growth_2026_why: Easing trade stress and fiscal support steady world growth
global_growth_2026_raw_wording: We expect global growth at 3.0%.
us_gdp_2026_why: Fiscal support and resilient demand lift U.S. growth
us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: We expect growth to accelerate to 2.2% in 2026 (above consensus), supported by looser fiscal policy and slightly lower, but still high, borrowing costs.
eurozone_gdp_2026_why: German fiscal impulse strengthens the euro area recovery
eurozone_gdp_2026_raw_wording: 2026 annual growth at 1.3%, slightly above the consensus of 1.1%.
china_gdp_2026_why: Further stimulus keeps China afloat through 2026
china_gdp_2026_raw_wording: Growth is expected to be close to 5% in 2025 slowing to around 4.5% in 2026.
us_inflation_2026_why: Tariffs lift U.S. core inflation early in 2026
us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: Tariff impacts will lift core PCE inflation to 3% yoy early next year, before giving way to gradual disinflation.
eurozone_inflation_2026_why: Eurozone inflation falls below target as wage pressure fades
eurozone_inflation_2026_raw_wording: Overall, annual inflation should fall to 1.8%, after 2.1% in 2025.
fed_year_end_rate_2026_why: Sticky inflation limits the pace of Fed easing
fed_year_end_rate_2026_raw_wording: We only expect one further rate cut by the Fed to a terminal rate at 3.25 -3.50%.
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_why: ECB stays on hold with rates in the neutral range
ecb_deposit_rate_year_end_2026_raw_wordi: With the 2.0% policy rate in the middle of the neutral range we do not expect key rate changes in 2026.
eur_usd_2026_why: Euro area green shoots lift EUR/USD
eur_usd_2026_raw_wording: The weighted average of BTP and OAT spreads over Bunds is consistent with EUR/USD closer to 1.20. Overall, we expect the EUR/USD to trend somewhat higher.
equities_overall_why: The house view is explicitly overweight equities.
equities_overall_raw_wording: Slight OW equities. Easy financial conditions and steady GDP growth ahead are fundamentally supportive.
equities_geography_us_why: US equities are less preferred on valuation grounds.
equities_geography_us_raw_wording: Over longer horizons, the US market appears overvalued compared to history. We recommend diversifying towards cheaper ex-US markets.
equities_geography_europe_why: Europe is preferred over the US in the regional equity mix.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: OW EMU versus US, Switzerland, DAX, and MDAX.
equities_geography_japan_why: Generali is neutral on Japan in the short term but remains overweight on a medium-term horizon thanks to fiscal stimulus, banks, tech momentum, and buybacks.
equities_geography_japan_raw_wording: Japan is neutral short term but still OW on a mid-term horizon
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: OW EM, still less expensive on PEG and with low positioning: Korea, Poland, China, and India.
equities_geography_china_why: China tech is a preferred diversifier within the equity allocation.
equities_geography_china_raw_wording: We suggest diversifying M7 exposure into AI beneficiaries and users, big China IT names, electrification and global nuclear.
fixed_income_overall_why: The asset-allocation mix clearly favors credit over sovereign safety.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: Our preference remains for Credit and Equities over Govies and Cash.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: positive
Investment-grade euro credit is a preferred fixed-income sleeve.
EA IG corporate bonds are preferred for their attractive carry and risk-return profile, especially compared to peripheral government bonds.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_w: High yield remains preferred in the current carry environment.
fixed_income_credit_quality_high_yield_r: HY credit and equities are still favoured, supported by resilient but unspectacular growth, easing inflation, and continued monetary policy support.
fixed_income_duration_why: Duration is kept short in euro fixed income.
fixed_income_duration_raw_wording: Government bonds are generally underweighted, with a moderately short duration recommended for euro area fixed income, as economic recovery and supply factors point to higher yields.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_wh: Sovereign duration is not the preferred bond exposure.
fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: Government bonds are generally underweighted.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_why: Corporate credit, especially euro credit, is preferred for carry.
fixed_income_segment_corporate_bonds_raw: Tech issuance will boost supply, putting a floor under IG spreads, but carry is attractive. We continue to prefer EUR Credit over US.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_why: EM sovereign debt remains a preferred yield segment.
fixed_income_segment_em_debt_raw_wording: We keep our preference for EM sovereign debt, as it continues to offer attractive relative valuations and positive return potential, with a small tilt to high yield names.
currencies_usd_why: Generali expects broad dollar depreciation.
currencies_usd_raw_wording: The USD remains fundamentally dear, and the persistently high twin deficits are posing a lasting burden. We expect the USD to trend weaker over 2026.
currencies_eur_why: The euro is expected to appreciate as euro-area data improves.
currencies_eur_raw_wording: The EUR has scope to catch up with economic green shoots in the euro area. Overall, we expect the EUR/USD to trend somewhat higher.
currencies_jpy_why: JPY is seen as undervalued with recovery potential.
currencies_jpy_raw_wording: JPY remains the most undervalued G10 currency and we expect the JPY to recover from its deep undervaluation in 2026.
currencies_em_fx_why: EM currencies are expected to strengthen further.
currencies_em_fx_raw_wording: EM FXs will appreciate further, and we favour high yielders.
themes_ai_why: AI remains investable, but Generali explicitly flags concentration and competition risk.
themes_ai_raw_wording: A mania may be developing, but it is too early to panic and underweight equities. We like to diversify into AI users, China Tech, or the electrification theme.
themes_healthcare_why: Healthcare and biotech are named as AI-adoption beneficiaries.
themes_healthcare_raw_wording: The technological diffusion to the broader economy will support profitability, with sectors like Financials, BioTech or even retail well positioned to benefit.
themes_energy_transition_why: Electrification is a named preferred structural theme.
themes_energy_transition_raw_wording: We like to diversify into AI users, China Tech, or the electrification theme.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Trade-war escalation and broader fragmentation remain explicit downside risks in the 2026 house view.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: a renewed escalation of the trade war with the US remains a key area to watch
themes_defense_sovereignty_why: Defense is explicitly one of the overweight European sector calls.
themes_defense_sovereignty_raw_wording: Overweight Financials, Defence and Software, along with Pharma, Retail, and Construction.

equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: complementing US exposures with allocations to Europe

Investment grade private credit to gain yield potential and diversification

fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: we still see a place for US Treasuries

global_growth_2026_raw_wording: The consensus among economists is for a year of moderate growth and inflation in 2026.

us_gdp_2026_raw_wording: US economic growth is relatively solid, and the combination of lower interest rates and tax cuts could provide tailwinds in the year ahead.
em_growth_2026_why: M&G expects emerging markets to remain structurally resilient with stronger growth and lower debt burdens than developed markets.
em_growth_2026_raw_wording: Emerging markets robust economic growth supported by a lower debt burden relative to developed markets.

us_inflation_2026_raw_wording: inflation will be closer to 3% than 2%.
equities_geography_us_why: M&G explicitly argues against maintaining a concentrated US overweight.
equities_geography_us_raw_wording: Policy volatility, trade frictions and concentration risk have prompted some institutional investors to consider diversifying away from an overweight US allocation.
equities_geography_europe_why: Europe is treated as a better-balanced equity region.
equities_geography_europe_raw_wording: Europe’s structural reforms are reinforcing its appeal as a region combining stability with growth potential.
equities_geography_em_why: Emerging markets are part of the valuation-driven diversification trade.
equities_geography_em_raw_wording: Investors may seek to diversify beyond expensive AI stocks and consider more attractively valued stocks in regions such as Europe and emerging markets.
fixed_income_overall_why: M&G sees bonds as offering good value again, with attractive real yields and active opportunities across global fixed income.
fixed_income_overall_raw_wording: Given the attractive real yields on offer across fixed income markets now globally, bonds offer good value compared to equities, in our view.
fixed_income_credit_quality_investment_g: ND

fixed_income_segment_government_bonds_ra: The real (inflation-adjusted) yields available on long-dated government bonds are more attractive than they have been in many years.

fixed_income_segment_em_debt_raw_wording: the emerging market debt universe appears an increasingly attractive alternative to US assets.
commodities_gold_why: Gold remains a preferred hedge asset.
commodities_gold_raw_wording: Gold, while lacking cash flow, could continue to serve as tail risk insurance in an environment of reduced dollar exposure and rising central bank demand.
themes_ai_why: AI is important, but M&G is explicitly cautious on passive exposure and valuation euphoria.
themes_ai_raw_wording: We are only at the very start of the widespread application of AI, but this is the time to let go of passive exposure to AI stocks and take a more active investment approach.
themes_healthcare_why: Health tech is named as one of the most compelling structural themes.
themes_healthcare_raw_wording: The most compelling opportunities sit within transformative sectors such as climate tech, fintech, cybersecurity and health tech.
themes_energy_transition_why: Energy transition remains a preferred long-duration theme.
themes_energy_transition_raw_wording: The most compelling opportunities sit within sectors innovating within energy transition and leading environmental and societal change.
themes_geopolitical_risk_why: Geopolitics remains a live source of volatility and risk.
themes_geopolitical_risk_raw_wording: Extreme volatility was all too often the prevailing backdrop: the Liberation Day tariffs crash, ongoing geopolitical conflict, a US credit rating downgrade and increasing questions about AI-led market momentum.
themes_other_why: Resilience is a recurring cross-asset theme in M&G’s outlook.
themes_other_raw_wording: Capital is moving toward certainty and resilience, favouring assets that can withstand volatility and deliver sustainable growth.
